Why it's bad: Generates additional SQL round-trips for bidirectional filter propagation. Can double the number of queries per visual.
May trigger unnecessary DirectQuery when Import would suffice. Dual tables are processed twice during refresh.
Forces full table scan in DirectQuery mode. Each FILTER(ALL) adds an unfiltered subquery to the generated SQL.
More columns increases bytes read per query. Primarily a cost issue; latency impact depends on whether I/O is the bottleneck.
No performance impact. Missing format strings affect display consistency and user experience.
Readability and maintenance issue. No performance impact but increases risk of errors when columns are renamed.
Hidden/unused columns are still included in DirectQuery SQL. Increases read bytes and cost per query.
MDX exposure on non-attribute columns forces extra metadata and memory structures. On large DirectQuery models this increases model load time and query overhead.
Time intelligence functions on DirectQuery tables generate multiple DQ queries per evaluation. On large fact tables this triggers full scans and severe latency.
Calculated tables re-evaluate on every refresh, block query caching, and increase model maintenance overhead.
Multi-hop DirectQuery joins prevent aggregation push-down and force chained subqueries. Each hop adds a nested SQL subquery.
Unmarked date tables disable time intelligence optimisations and prevent efficient date filter caching in the storage engine.
M functions that block query folding force full data pulls from the source instead of pushing transformations server-side.

Action: Fix 6 E02 (Missing liquid clustering on large curated tables) violations
Required action: Add liquid_clustered_by to the contract YAML with the primary filter columns.

Action: Fix 2 E04 (No WHERE filter on serve view over large fact table) violations
Required action: Add date-based WHERE filter to restrict the scan window.

Action: Fix 6 E05 (Functions on filter columns) violations
Required action: Remove functions from filter columns; pre-compute values in the model instead.

Action: Fix 6 E07 (ROW_NUMBER subselect instead of QUALIFY) violations
Required action: Replace the ROW_NUMBER subquery pattern with QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER(...) = 1.

Action: Fix 5 E13 (Hardcoded magic numbers in SQL) violations
Required action: Use dbt variables or named constants for business-rule thresholds.

Action: Fix 2 E15 (Serve view that should be materialised_view) violations
Required action: Change materialisation from 'view' to 'materialized_view' in the contract YAML.

Action: Fix 12 V01 (Too many visuals per page) findings in Trade
Why it's bad: 28 visual containers (threshold: 15)
Required action: Split into sub-pages or use bookmarks to show/hide groups of visuals

Action: Fix 1 V03 (Excessive report-level filters) findings in Trade
Why it's bad: 35 report-level filters (threshold: 10)
Required action: Consolidate report-level filters. Move page-specific filters to page level

Action: Fix 1 V05 (Auto-refresh or query reduction settings) findings in Trade
Why it's bad: Query reduction settings not fully optimised: Cross-highlighting enabled — hovering on visuals generates additional DirectQuery queries; Slicer 'Apply' button disabled — each slicer selection immediately fires queries instead of batching; Filter 'Apply' button disabled — each filter change immediately fires queries
Required action: In PBI Desktop → File → Options → Report settings → Query reduction: consider disabling cross-highlighting and enabling 'Apply' buttons on slicers and filters to reduce DirectQuery query volume during user interaction.

Action: Fix 19 V06 (Wide tables/matrices) findings in Trade
Why it's bad: matrix visual '0eac3eb0a1d581031ba4' has 43 projected columns (threshold: 10)
Required action: Reduce column count to essential fields. Use drillthrough pages for detail views

Action: Fix 12 V08 (Large embedded images) findings in Trade
Why it's bad: 1 image visual(s)
Required action: Use URL-referenced images hosted on a CDN instead of embedded images. Compress backgrounds to <200 KB

Why it's bad: FILTER(ALL()) materialises the entire column into memory before filtering row by row. In DirectQuery this generates a full table scan subquery.
Required action: Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Column]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Column]) and a direct predicate in CALCULATE

Why it's bad: Contains 3 CALCULATE calls creating multiple context transitions. In DirectQuery, each nesting level generates an additional SQL subquery.
Required action: Extract inner calculations into VAR variables or separate measures to reduce nesting

Why it's bad: Without VAR, the same subexpression is recalculated every time it appears, potentially doubling or tripling query cost.
Required action: Use VAR to capture the repeated expression and reference the variable instead

Why it's bad: Without an explicit format string, PBI uses raw floating-point display, causing inconsistencies across visuals.
Required action: Add a formatString (e.g. '#,0.00' for numbers, '0.0%' for percentages, '#,0' for integers)

Why it's bad: DIVIDE(CALCULATE(...), ...) forces the engine to evaluate the CALCULATE separately from the denominator, preventing single-pass aggregation. Each part generates its own DQ query.
Required action: Use VAR to pre-compute CALCULATE result, then DIVIDE the variable

Why it's bad: Unqualified column names can resolve ambiguously when tables share column names.
Required action: Use 'TableName'[ColumnName] instead of bare [ColumnName]

Why it's bad: Without VAR, intermediate calculations may be re-evaluated multiple times. VARs guarantee single evaluation.
Required action: Use VAR/RETURN pattern to name intermediate calculations for readability and potential performance gains

Why it's bad: Hardcoded literals make measures fragile and hard to maintain. Changes require editing each measure individually.
Required action: Extract hardcoded values to a parameter table or separate measure for maintainability
